name: Seinfeld
description: A show about nothing — Jerry, Elaine, George, and Kramer

questions.q: What is Kramer's first name, which he hides for most of the series?

source.notes: Revealed in 'The Switch' (Season 6). He insists on being called just 'Kramer.'

questions.q: What fake profession does George Costanza claim most often when trying to impress women?

source.notes: Sometimes claims to have designed the addition to the Guggenheim.

questions.q: What is George's alter-ego name used in his business schemes?

source.notes: An importer/exporter by trade — though the details keep changing.

questions.q: What is Elaine's frequently-used catchphrase for skipping over the details of a story?

source.notes: 'The Yada Yada' episode aired in Season 8 (1997).

questions.q: What iconic shirt does Jerry wear on the Today Show (and regret)?

source.notes: Now on display at the Smithsonian's National Museum of American History.

questions.q: What is the name of the strict soup-shop owner who demands proper ordering etiquette?

source.notes: Based on real NYC soup vendor Al Yeganeh. Catchphrase: 'No soup for you!'

questions.q: Which two babka flavors are famously compared in the show, with cinnamon deemed the 'lesser'?

source.notes: 'The Dinner Party' (Season 5).

questions.q: What is the name of the NYC catalog company where Elaine works under a quirky adventurer boss?

source.original_a: He is the eccentric owner of the J Peterman catalog where Elaine works.
source.notes: J. Peterman is a real catalog company. John O'Hurley played the fictional Peterman.

questions.q: Which New York Mets baseball star famously guest-stars and dates Elaine in multiple episodes?

source.notes: First appeared in the two-part 'The Boyfriend' episode (Season 3).

questions.q: Which NYC diner's exterior shots are used for 'Monk's Café' on the show?

source.notes: Located at Broadway and 112th Street, near Columbia University.

questions.q: According to Jerry, what snack broke his 13-year streak of not eating any until he had one?

source.notes: 'The Dinner Party.' Jerry says 'Look to the cookie!'

questions.q: George's famous break-up line is what?

source.notes: Later turned on him when a girlfriend uses it on him first.

questions.q: In which specific area of New York City does Jerry Seinfeld's apartment supposedly sit?

source.notes: Apartment 5A at 129 West 81st Street (fictional). Real address is 757 S. New Hampshire Ave. in LA.

questions.q: What is the Latvian Orthodox term Kramer uses for his irresistible 'power' over women?

source.notes: 'The Conversion' (Season 5). The priest declares Kramer has 'the kavorka.'

questions.q: How many seasons of 'Seinfeld' aired on NBC in total?

source.notes: Aired July 1989 to May 1998. 180 episodes.

questions.q: In 'The Marine Biologist' episode, George's fake career turns out to be useful when he finds what on the beach?

source.notes: George's monologue: 'The sea was angry that day, my friends...'

questions.q: In 'The Chinese Restaurant,' what action do the characters spend the entire episode doing?

source.notes: Filmed in real-time. NBC executives initially hated it.

questions.q: What unique storytelling technique is used in the episode 'The Betrayal'?

source.notes: Season 9. Homage to Harold Pinter's play of the same name.

questions.q: Who co-created Seinfeld alongside Jerry Seinfeld?

source.notes: David wrote most early episodes and inspired the George Costanza character.

questions.q: What is the name of Elaine's unreliable dance style that becomes a running joke?

source.notes: George describes it as 'a full-body-dry-heave set to music' in 'The Little Kicks.'
